what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment linked to a channel memory entry in the design-source channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no prose body
Target is an image:// URI pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-04
Includes an added timestamp in ISO 8601
File id is att-15a69835 and channel is design-source
standouts: Image attachment metadata for pitch image p-04
Uses an image:// namespaced URI as the target
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Target uses a structured URI scheme rather than a raw filesystem path
concerns: No alt text, caption, or description for the image
The image:// URI cannot be resolved from this file alone
Empty body provides no additional context
notes: A sparse metadata stub that registers an image attachment for a recent pitch in the design-source channel.
questions: What is pitch p-04 and where is its corresponding channel memory entry?
Where is the actual image asset stored and how is the image:// URI resolved?
Who added this attachment and why?
Does attachment_type images imply a specific format or set of formats?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a metadata record for an image attachment, which is exactly what its frontmatter fields describe.
intention_signals: Register an image asset as an attachment to a design-source channel memory item so it can be referenced and retrieved by its target URI.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI scheme to address an attachment target instead of a conventional filesystem path.
contribution: It provides a durable, timestamped metadata record linking a channel memory entry to an image asset via a namespaced URI, enabling attachment discovery and provenance tracking.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter metadata stub that registers an image attachment with target image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-04 in the design-source channel memory.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to record and expose the existence and location of a pitch image attached to a design-source channel memory entry.
